title: Inbox vs. Notifications
excerpt: I don’t use the inbox of notifications at all because those tabs feel like they’re both overlapping - what’s the difference?  Since I don’t have a clear understanding of how they differ, they aren’t useful for me at all.…

posts.author: keames (K.C. Eames)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t use the inbox of notifications at all because those tabs feel like they’re both overlapping - what’s the difference? Since I don’t have a clear understanding of how they differ, they aren’t useful for me at all. Does anyone else have this feeling? It seems like an odd duplication or minor overlapping of information so neither end up being that useful and I end up relying on email notifications to know what to do next.

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m noticing the same issue. There is a lot of overlap and wonder what we can do to fix this

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think the latest update on the bel-notifications section is a huge improvement. Being able to select between the different types of notifications is really helpful for me and the team.

image1176×258 18.2 KB

My feedback on the new way of showing notifications is that the All and Unread are (for me) most of the time basically the same notifications. The Unread section for me personally is not very useful. Maybe it is for others. I personally really like the ‘I was mentioned’ and ‘Assigned to me’. If you ask me the ‘I was mentioned’ can be the first one in the row because for me this is the main reason to go to the bel in the first place.
On top of that it would be great when the ‘I was mentioned and did not reply’ functionality will be implemented here as well. This one is very useful.

To elaborate on the bell vs. the inbox. I agree there can be some improvement on the understanding of how they differ.

DisplaysExtra_and_Item-0_and_AppleVPNExtra_png528×672 42.3 KB

For me the suggestion below would be a logical setup for the notifications and updates I could think of being used in Monday

Bell notifications - personal and straight-away-action-needed notifications

  • When you are mentioned (already exists)
  • When assigned to item (already exists)
  • Your reminders (the reminders that you set on updates should be notified here because you wanted to be reminded. I am not sure if the reminders already pop up here to be honest)
  • Due date expired on item (were you are assigned as assignee or person)
  • Likes on your update (could be tab number 4 in the bel notification)
  • Private messages (future feature - when one-on-one chat functionality is being implemented )

Inbox - information about board activity
Inbox could exist out of three parts:

  1. Updates
    The main part of this section should include everything around updates on a board that you follow:
  • New item is created on a board that you follow (shown as a number; 25 items are created in the last period of time)
  • New update is added to an item (grouped based on the item that the updates are added to so it straight away offers context)
  • New (or latest) comment / reply on a update

It can be possible the inbox will show a lot of updates with the three type of updates above, but you always have the possibility to select the updates per board via the filter on the right.

Also I would love to see the functionality which you can compare to Expand the Pulse Pop-up View (Link to item Column) - #3 by Eltjo being added on the items updates, this would give you the possibility to edit items from your inbox.

  1. Notifications
    Notifications which do not require action from your side perse, but are good to be aware of
  • New board is created
  • Subscription to board
  • Deleted items
  • Archived items
  • Bookmarked updates (already exists)
  • Etc
  1. Filter

The inbox section already has filter options to filter per board and its updates. I would love to see an addition here, to be able to see all the new added items on each board as wel.(see also the suggestion /#/t/2662 on being able to filter on new added items on boards).

Related topics:

Inbox - I was mentioned options Feature requests
I am aware that you can use the inbox to view only items that you were mentioned in. However, this only includes items that you were mentioned in at the start of an update. If someone @ you in a back and forth in updates, it will not show up in the inbox. Shouldn’t this show up there? It is a really easy to track all communication on a pulse…not just the first time someone @ me if the inbox was set up to track everything that I was mentioned in. As a manager, following all of the items that I ne…

/#/t/2662

Hope this all makes sense, it would definitely work for me. Cheers

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Thanks for circling back to this issue. Our team is considering and collecting feedback on the Inbox to revamp this in Q4. Adding replies to updates “I was mentioned” is not a quick win in terms of effort so we are looking critically at all components to improve this area in general as well as the relationship with Bell Notifications. Once we have made some progress with clearer picture, I’ll be sure to share In the meantime, check out the “I was mentioned” widget on our dashboards. You can create an entire dashboard for communication using this widget as well as board updates. Keep the feedback coming our way - it is so valuable!
Cheers,
Laura

posts.author: Wesley (Wesley Gant)
posts.cookedHtml:
@lauralev These are nice updates. Confusion around notifications and inbox has been a huge pain point with our team, who defaults to using Slack because it’s easier and they don’t miss anything. To make a huge leap forward, I really think the monday team should consider removing the “inbox” entirely and turn notifications into a larger, more powerful messaging space.

This space would organize two types of messages: Conversations and Notifications.

Conversations would be @mentions that I haven’t replied to as well as any other messaging updates on boards I’m following. When my team of humans is trying to communicate, that should take priority.

Notifications would include all the various little things that are generated by automations and triggers. They’re usually less important, but they can become overwhelming and get in the way.

For every item, the user should be able to set a reminder, mark as read, like, or delete.

Above each group, some simple filtering tools to view “Unread” or “Mark all as read” or even “View last 7 days” would be great.

posts.author: Zoltan.Nagy (Zoltan)
posts.cookedHtml: Dear community and Monday Team. Are there any development in this issue? My team also confused, everything is fine in Monday, but the inbox in our team heads also related to “conversations”, so the mentions and replyes would be logical for us to include in inbox as Wesley Gant and others suggested. Very frustrated. This conversation is about development in 2020 Q4? So this suggestion was totally rejected? Or please suggest some settings if you can? Thanks Zoltan

posts.author: Ferrugius (Jakub Kalinowski)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 There is way too much overlap between inbox and notifications. It should be unified.

posts.author: SHK (Sam Hancock)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree. I’m confused by it too and starting to resent processing the same information twice.

posts.author: alskop (Al Skop)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in the same boat here. Maybe it’s the way I have notifications set up but bell and inbox are practically mirror images of each other.

posts.author: themondaypro (Jesse Hernandez)
posts.cookedHtml: Commenting to share my support for this feature request. Clients have a hard time understanding the difference between the Inbox and Notifications, and why this information is separated, considering the amount of overlap. I think it would be a good idea to combine the inbox and the notifications into one feed.

posts.author: srosato (Steven Rosato)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for unified notifications and inbox

If we are not to unify notifications and inbox (although I think that would be better to unify), it would be nice that if we go click on a notification that brings us to an update and read it (you can tell its read when you have the “seen by” option on the update), that it removes it from the inbox to avoid that duplication.

posts.author: cbrunskill (Catherine Brunskill)
posts.cookedHtml: coming in to also show my support for this. It’s annoying to have to mark something as “read” in two places. Why can’t the “read” status at least sync across both Inbox and Notifications so that you have accurate indicators of how many “new” items you have (in either space). A thousand times yes please look into this Monday.com team!
